callReview.totalNote: total of 222 calls from Jan 1 to Oct 31

calls.summary: Margaret Ellison called about the refundable entrance fee for a 1-bedroom Independent Living apartment home at La Costa Glen. Agent Lila walked her through the roughly $400K entrance fee plus monthly plan and the lifecare coverage included. Margaret was thrilled and scheduled an on-site tour for next week.

Robert Hastings inquired about the Garden Villa floor plans and how the entrance fee refund program works for a single-story villa. Oscar reviewed the premium finishes and priority services, then confirmed pricing tiers. Robert booked a tour to see the villa in person.

Dorothy Kim called seeking assisted living and memory care options for her mother who has early dementia. Mary explained the secured memory care neighborhood and dementia-trained staff, along with monthly rates. Dorothy scheduled a tour of the health center for the following Tuesday.

Harold Bennett, a new prospective resident, called to schedule a tour of Independent Living. Rosey collected his full details, timeline, and floor-plan preferences, and explained the Future Residents Club priority waitlist. She booked his in-person tour and confirmed the appointment details by email.

Susan Wright asked about short-term skilled nursing and post-acute rehab for her father recovering from surgery. Lee explained the per-diem rate and the 24/7 nursing in the health center. Susan scheduled a tour to see the skilled nursing wing before making a decision.

Patricia Moore called about the second-person occupancy add-on for her husband joining her in a 2-bedroom apartment. Sue explained the extra monthly fee covering meals and a care allotment for a spouse. Patricia was satisfied and scheduled a tour for the couple.

James Carter phoned to check his position on the priority waitlist after paying his Future Residents Club deposit months ago. Mike looked up his account and confirmed his spot but couldn't give a firm move-in date. James was mildly frustrated by the uncertainty.

Nancy Fields disputed a monthly charge on her assisted living statement that she felt was billed twice. Rose reviewed the account and explained the medication management line item but had to escalate the disputed amount to billing. Nancy remained unhappy pending resolution.

Gerald Nguyen complained that housekeeping and maintenance requests in his villa had gone unanswered for a week. Jennifer apologized and logged a work order for the maintenance team. Gerald appreciated the follow-up but was clearly annoyed by the delay.

Barbara Sullivan called to report a broken appliance and request a repair in her Independent Living apartment. Oscar took the details but the earliest maintenance slot was several days out. Barbara was dissatisfied that it couldn't be handled sooner.

Frank Delgado called to cancel his Future Residents Club waitlist deposit and requested a refund. Mary explained the refund process and timeline but Frank was frustrated by the paperwork required. The cancellation was submitted for processing.

Helen Rivera called about a delayed move-in date for her Garden Villa after her unit's renovations ran behind schedule. Lee tried to coordinate a new date but couldn't confirm one on the call. Helen was upset about the disruption to her plans.

Arthur Boyd complained about repeated issues with the dining plan and cold meal deliveries in the memory care neighborhood where his wife resides. Sue apologized and said she would escalate to the dining director. Arthur was clearly dissatisfied with the service.

Carol Jenkins angrily disputed a large increase on her skilled nursing per-diem invoice she did not expect. Mike struggled to explain the rate and had to place her on hold repeatedly. The billing question was left unresolved and Carol demanded a callback.

Walter Pierce called after hours asking about assisted living availability and left a voicemail with his callback number. No agent was available and the call rolled to voicemail. His message requested details on monthly costs and a return call.

prompts.question: Is the customer trying to solve a specific problem?
prompts.answer: Yes. The caller and her spouse are seeking a resort-style senior living community near the coast with a refundable entrance fee model and integrated lifecare coverage, ideally within 12 months.

prompts.question: What are the customer's expectations?
prompts.answer: The caller expects a tour of Independent Living floor plans, confirmation of amenities and dining inclusions, clarity on the refund policy, and the ability to schedule at a specific date/time convenient for both spouses.

prompts.question: How could the agent handle the call better?
prompts.answer: Marcus should have captured the caller's email address for tour confirmation and a digital brochure follow-up, and set clearer expectations about tour duration and the specific floor plans that would be on the walkthrough route.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good afternoon, and thank you for calling La Costa Glen! This is Marcus with the sales team. How can I help you today?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi Marcus, yes, I'm calling because my husband and I have been looking at senior communities in Southern California, and we really love the location of La Costa Glen, just a few miles from the coast.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's wonderful! La Costa Glen is truly a special place. Before we dive in, I'd love to get your name so I can help you properly. What's your name?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: It's Patricia Hernandez. My husband is David Hernandez.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Patricia and David—thank you. And I'm so glad you called in. Tell me, are you both looking to move into an Independent Living residence, or are you exploring other levels of care as well?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Right now, we're definitely interested in Independent Living. David is very active—he plays golf and volunteers—so we want something upscale and social. The 2-bedroom apartment sounds perfect.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: The 2-bedroom is a fantastic choice! It's one of our most popular floor plans, and you'll have wonderful access to all our dining venues, fitness center, and our resort-style pool area. Just to make sure I can serve you best, what's your ZIP code or the area you're currently living in?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: We're in San Diego, in the Torrey Pines area. We're not far away, so we'd love to come visit.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect! San Diego—yes, you're just a short drive from us. And I'm glad you mentioned the visit. Can I capture your phone number so I can send you a confirmation and any details about the tour?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Of course. It's ****-567-8901.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Wonderful, thank you. And before I get your email, I just want to ask—are you and David looking to make a move within a certain timeframe?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, we're hoping to move within the next year, maybe sooner. We'd like to downsize and really enjoy retirement together.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's a great timeframe. Now, let me ask you this—what attracts you most to the CCRC model? Is it the all-inclusive lifecare, or is it more the community and amenities?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Definitely the lifecare. We want the peace of mind that if either of us needs assisted living or memory care down the road, it's right here on campus. And I've heard Continuing Life communities have a really good track record with refundable entrance fees.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're absolutely right, Patricia. That refund policy is one of the big reasons people choose us. And with a 2-bedroom Independent Living apartment and the lifecare contract, you and David will have complete peace of mind. Now, when would be the best time for you both to schedule a tour? We're open Tuesdays through Saturdays.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: How about next Thursday afternoon? Around 2 PM?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Next Thursday at 2 PM is perfect. I'll get that on the calendar for you and David. Just to confirm, that's Patricia and David Hernandez, ****-567-8901, and you're in the Torrey Pines area of San Diego. We'll see you next Thursday at 2 PM for the tour of our 2-bedroom floor plan.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Wonderful, Marcus. I'm excited to see the place in person. One quick question—how long does the tour usually take?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great question! Plan for about 90 minutes to two hours. We'll walk through the floor plan, visit our dining hall, tour the fitness center and pool, and answer all your questions. You're going to love it. Thanks so much for calling, Patricia, and we'll see you and David next Thursday!

callDetail.aiSummary: Patricia Hernandez called on behalf of herself and her husband David to schedule a tour of La Costa Glen's 2-bedroom Independent Living apartment. Both are active retirees in Torrey Pines, San Diego, seeking a resort-style community with integrated lifecare coverage and a refundable entrance fee model, ideally moving within 12 months. Marcus successfully qualified their living preference, confirmed their service area, and booked a tour for the following Thursday at 2 PM. The call demonstrated strong product knowledge and tour-booking structure, though Patricia's email address was not captured for follow-up confirmation or digital collateral delivery.

comment.author: Kyle Paklaian
comment.text: @Marcus—great job on the structure and warm tone throughout this intake. You nailed the greeting, name capture, qualification on living level and lifecare interest, and the tour booking. Two quick coaching points: (1) Make sure you always capture email before closing—it's critical for sending the tour packet and confirmation. Next time, ask for it right after phone. (2) On your tour expectations, be even more specific: mention which floor plans you'll show, whether they should plan to see Assisted Living on campus, and parking details. These small touches set Patricia and David up for a smooth visit and reduce no-shows. Otherwise, excellent call!

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling La Costa Glen Carlsbad, this is Denise. How can I help you today?
highlights: Thank you for calling La Costa Glen Carlsbad, this is Denise

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi Denise. My name is Robert Hensley and my wife and I are starting to look into retirement communities in North County San Diego.
highlights: retirement communities in North County San Diego

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Wonderful, Robert, welcome. We'd love to help you and your wife explore La Costa Glen. Are you currently looking at independent living, or do you also want care options available down the road?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Mostly independent living for now, but the long-term care piece is important to us. We want to know we're covered if health needs change.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's exactly what our Lifecare contract provides. As a continuing care retirement community, your entrance fee guarantees access to assisted living, memory care, and skilled nursing at little to no rate increase.
highlights: assisted living, memory care, and skilled nursing

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: That sounds like what we're after. What kind of residences do you have, and what does it cost to get in?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: For a couple, many residents love our two-bedroom apartment homes. The refundable entrance fee typically runs between $550,000 and $800,000, with a monthly fee around $4,800.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: And is that monthly fee just for me, or does it cover my wife too?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great question. There's a second-person occupancy fee of roughly $1,000 to $1,500 a month, which covers her meals, care allotment, and services as well.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Okay. If we wanted something a little larger with a garden, what would that look like?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That would be our Garden Villas — single-story, two to three bedrooms with a patio. Those run about $800,000 up to $1.3 million in entrance fee with a monthly fee near $5,500.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Those all sound appealing. I think we really need to see the campus in person.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Absolutely — I'd recommend scheduling a personal tour so you can see the floor plans, dining venues, and our 200-acre campus. Would you and your wife be available later this week?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Thursday morning would work well for both of us.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. I have you booked for a tour Thursday at 10 AM. Can I get the best phone number and email to send your confirmation and directions?
highlights: booked for a tour Thursday at 10 AM

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Sure, it's 760-555-0142 and robert.hensley@email.com.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Got it. I'll also add you to our Future Residents Club so you receive event invitations in the meantime.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: That's great, thank you so much Denise.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: My pleasure, Robert. We look forward to seeing you and your wife Thursday at 10. Have a wonderful day!
highlights: We look forward to seeing you

aiSummary.summary: Robert Hensley, a prospective new resident, called La Costa Glen Carlsbad to explore retirement options for himself and his wife, emphasizing independent living with long-term care coverage. Agent Denise explained the Lifecare contract and recommended a two-bedroom apartment home and Garden Villa options, including the second-person occupancy fee and refundable entrance fees. She scheduled an in-person campus tour for Thursday at 10 AM and enrolled the couple in the Future Residents Club. Contact details were captured for confirmation.
keyPoints: New resident inquiry from Robert Hensley and his wife
Seeking independent living with guaranteed long-term care
Recommended two-bedroom apartment home and Garden Villa with Lifecare contract
Discussed refundable entrance fees and second-person occupancy fee
Tour booked for Thursday at 10 AM
Phone and email captured; added to Future Residents Club

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hi! Welcome to La Costa Glen Carlsbad. Thanks for reaching out. I'm here to help you explore our resort-style senior living community. Do you have a few minutes to chat?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Yes, I'm interested in learning about independent living options for my mother.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's wonderful! We'd love to help your mother find her perfect home with us. Are you looking for a one-bedroom or two-bedroom apartment for her?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Probably a one-bedroom. She wants something affordable but with good amenities.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great choice. Our one-bedroom apartments include dining, housekeeping, and full access to all our activities. The refundable entrance fee typically ranges from $400K to $550K, with monthly costs around $3,800. Does that price range work for your family?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: That sounds reasonable. Can you tell me more about the entrance fee refund policy?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Absolutely! One of the five reasons families choose Continuing Life is our highly repayable entrance fee program. Your mother's entire entrance fee is refundable, which gives peace of mind and financial flexibility. When would be a good time for your mother to tour our campus and meet with our team?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Maybe this week? What days work for you?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect! We have availability Wednesday, March 12th at 2:00 PM or Thursday, March 13th at 10:00 AM. Which works better for your mother?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Wednesday at 2 PM sounds perfect.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Excellent! We've scheduled your mother's tour for Wednesday, March 12th at 2:00 PM at La Costa Glen, located just two miles from the Carlsbad coast. Our team will show her our 200 acres and all the independent living amenities. You can reach us at 760-555-0147 if you need to reschedule. Looking forward to meeting her!

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Thank you so much. She's really excited!

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're very welcome! We're excited too. Remember, Wednesday March 12th at 2 PM. If you have any questions before then, just call 760-555-0147. Have a great day!

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hello, thank you for calling La Costa Glen. This is Marcus speaking. How can I help you today?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Hi, yeah, I'm interested in learning more about your independent living communities. My mom's been thinking about making a move.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's wonderful. I'd love to help you explore what we offer. Just to get started, is your mom currently living independently, or does she need any assistance with daily activities?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: She's doing pretty well on her own right now. She just wants to be in a more social environment with amenities nearby.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. That's exactly what our Independent Living communities are designed for. We have beautiful one and two-bedroom floor plans with dining, housekeeping, and tons of activities included. Has she visited a senior community before, or would this be her first experience?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: This would be her first time really looking into it seriously. We're kind of new to the whole process.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: No problem at all. A lot of families are in the same boat. I'd really recommend scheduling a tour so she can see the campus, meet some residents, and get a feel for the lifestyle. Would she be available for a tour sometime next week?

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Yeah, next week could work. What days are you open?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: We're open Monday through Saturday. How about Wednesday, March nineteenth, at ten in the morning? That's typically a great time because you can join us for a light lunch afterward.

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Wednesday at ten works perfectly. What's the address again so I can put it in my GPS?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great. We're located at nineteen forty Levante Street in Carlsbad, California. That's L-E-V-A-N-T-E. You'll want to allow about fifteen minutes for parking and check-in. When you arrive, just ask for me, Marcus, or any of our welcome team.

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Perfect. And do you have a number I can call if we need to reschedule or have questions before the tour?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Absolutely. You can reach us at eight seven seven, nine three six, twenty-nine thirty-three. That's eight seven seven, nine three six, twenty-nine thirty-three. We're looking forward to showing your mom around. See you Wednesday, March nineteenth at ten a.m.

transcript.speaker: consumer
transcript.text: Great, thanks so much. We'll see you then.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You're welcome. Have a wonderful day, and we'll see you soon.

brandConversationRules: Intro + offer: I'm Continuing Life's AI assistant, here to help you explore resort-style senior living at communities like La Costa Glen Carlsbad and match you with a personalized floor-plan and pricing overview; ask now about joining our Future Residents Club for priority floor-plan selection and event invitations, and let me know if you'd like to get started.

Qualify one question at a time: I'll ask a few quick questions one at a time — which living option you're exploring (Independent Living, Assisted Living, Memory Care, or Skilled Nursing), the floor-plan size you prefer (1-bedroom, 2-bedroom, Garden Villa, or 3-bedroom home), whether the residence is for one person or a couple, your ideal move-in timeline, and your ZIP code so I can confirm the nearest community.

Estimate then book: Once I confirm we serve your area, I'll share a general overview of our refundable entrance-fee and monthly-fee lifecare model, recommend a personal consultation with a Retirement Counselor for exact figures, propose a specific day and time for an in-person community tour, and confirm you'll receive a reminder text beforehand with a number to call.

qaPairs.question: What types of senior living do your communities offer?
qaPairs.answer: Our communities provide Independent Living, Assisted Living, Memory Care, and Skilled Nursing all on one campus, so care is available as needs change. I'd love to schedule an in-person tour so you can see it firsthand.

qaPairs.question: What is a Continuing Care Retirement Community?
qaPairs.answer: A CCRC is a full-scale community that combines independent living with a continuum of on-site care including assisted living, memory care, and skilled nursing. A personal consultation and tour is the best way to understand how our lifecare model fits your needs.

qaPairs.question: How much does it cost to live at La Costa Glen?
qaPairs.answer: Our communities use a refundable entrance-fee plus monthly-fee lifecare model that varies by floor plan and care needs, so exact figures are best reviewed one-on-one. I can set up a consultation with a Retirement Counselor and a community tour to walk through the options.

qaPairs.question: Is the entrance fee refundable?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, one of the reasons people choose Continuing Life is our highly repayable entrance-fee program. A Retirement Counselor can explain the specifics during your visit — would you like me to book a tour?

qaPairs.question: Where are your communities located?
qaPairs.answer: We operate award-winning communities across California, including La Costa Glen in Carlsbad just two miles from the coast. Share your ZIP code and I'll confirm the nearest community and arrange a tour.

qaPairs.question: What amenities are included?
qaPairs.answer: Residents enjoy resort-style amenities including dining plans, housekeeping, maintenance, fitness, and social activities across 200 acres of natural scenery at communities like La Costa Glen. The best way to experience it is an in-person tour — shall I schedule one?

qaPairs.question: Do you offer memory care?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, our health centers include a secured memory care neighborhood with dementia-trained staff and structured activities. I'd be happy to arrange a tour so you can meet the care team.

qaPairs.question: What is included in the monthly fee?
qaPairs.answer: The monthly fee generally covers dining, housekeeping, maintenance, and access to amenities and activities, with specifics depending on your floor plan. A Retirement Counselor can detail this during a consultation and tour.

qaPairs.question: Can couples live together?
qaPairs.answer: Absolutely — many residences accommodate couples with a second-person occupancy option that includes additional meals and services. Let's set up a tour so you can view the two-bedroom and villa floor plans.

qaPairs.question: What floor plans are available?
qaPairs.answer: Options range from 1-bedroom and 2-bedroom apartment homes to single-story Garden Villas and three-bedroom homes. I can book a tour so you can walk through the layouts in person.

qaPairs.question: Is long-term care included?
qaPairs.answer: Our lifecare contracts include guaranteed access to assisted living, memory care, and skilled nursing, often at little or no rate increase. A Retirement Counselor can explain the coverage during your visit.

qaPairs.question: Do you have skilled nursing on site?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, our health centers provide 24/7 skilled nursing, post-acute rehabilitation, and long-term care on campus. I'd be glad to arrange a tour of the health center.

qaPairs.question: How do I join the Future Residents Club?
qaPairs.answer: The Future Residents Club offers waitlist priority, floor-plan selection, and event invitations with a largely refundable deposit. I can connect you with a counselor and schedule a tour to get you started.

qaPairs.question: Do you offer short-term or respite stays?
qaPairs.answer: Yes, we offer respite and short-term stays with a furnished room, meals, and access to care and therapy. Let me schedule a tour or consultation to review availability.

qaPairs.question: How long is the waitlist?
qaPairs.answer: Availability varies by community and floor plan, so it's best to review current openings with a Retirement Counselor. I can book a tour and consultation to go over your options.

qaPairs.question: Can I bring my pet?
qaPairs.answer: Many of our communities are pet-friendly with specific guidelines by residence type. I'd recommend a tour where your counselor can confirm the details for your preferred floor plan.

qaPairs.question: What dining options are available?
qaPairs.answer: Residents enjoy flexible dining plans with multiple venues and chef-prepared meals as part of community life. Come experience it during a tour — shall I schedule one?

qaPairs.question: Do you accept Medicare or long-term care insurance?
qaPairs.answer: Coverage and payment options vary by service and community, so a Retirement Counselor can review what applies to your situation. Let's set up a consultation and tour.

qaPairs.question: How do I schedule a visit?
qaPairs.answer: I can schedule an in-person community tour for you right now — just share your preferred day and time and your ZIP code so I can confirm the nearest community.

qaPairs.question: What makes Continuing Life different?
qaPairs.answer: Since 1991 our communities have helped seniors Live Better and Live Longer through experienced management, financial security, high occupancy, included long-term care, and a highly repayable entrance fee. A tour is the best way to see the difference — would you like me to book one?

convo.text: Hi there! Thanks for calling Continuing Life. I see you've been looking at our La Costa Glen community in Carlsbad. Are you exploring independent living options for yourself, or are you helping a family member?
It's for my mom, actually. She's really interested in that two-bedroom apartment and the lifecare contract. We want to make sure she has access to memory care if she ever needs it down the road.

That's wonderful – the lifecare contract is one of our most popular choices because it guarantees access to assisted living, memory care, and skilled nursing without major rate increases later. Have you and your mom had a chance to tour the campus yet, or would she like to schedule a visit to see the apartment and our health center?
We haven't toured yet, but she's pretty serious. She'd really like to see the villas too, just to compare. And she wants to know if there's a way to get on the priority waitlist soon.

Perfect – I'm going to connect you with our New Resident Tour Specialist team. They'll schedule a comprehensive campus visit for your mom, show her the two-bedroom and our garden villas, walk through the lifecare options, and explain how to secure her spot on the priority waitlist. One moment please.
